note: Web wallet: clarify RPC modes and block read-only broadcast endpoint. The RPC bar at the top of sost-wallet.html now shows a coloured badge next to the connect button: LOCAL NODE for 127.0.0.1/localhost, READ ONLY for /rpc/public, AUTH REQUIRED for /rpc, CUSTOM for anything else. A new ? button toggles a 3-row reference panel explaining what each endpoint does and a clarification that 127.0.0.1 means 'this computer' (the user's own sost-node), not the SOST website server. sendTransaction and sendTransactionMany now refuse to sign + broadcast when the active endpoint is classified READ_ONLY, with a clear error pointing the user at either http://127.0.0.1:18232 or /rpc with credentials. No nginx changes were made automatically; the commit message includes the diagnostic curl recipe operators can run themselves to verify that /rpc and http://127.0.0.1:18232 both reject a junk TX with -22 (TX decode), not -401 (auth required). Force-reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
